questions.question: What are the 4 conditions that must ALL be met for a prototype customer to qualify for a free upgrade?

options.text: Feedback within 30 days, official form, any phone number, visible in Shopify
Feedback within 45 days of delivery, official Sylvi Prototype Feedback Form, same phone number as purchase, feedback visible in FIMS
Feedback within 45 days of purchase, any feedback channel, same email as purchase, visible in FIMS
Feedback within 60 days of delivery, official form, same phone number, approved by Manager

questions.question: What is the FIMS path to verify whether a customer's prototype feedback form has been submitted?

options.text: FIMS → OMS → Shopify → Order Search → Feedback tab
FIMS → Tools → Prototype Reviews
FIMS → OMS → RMA → Prototype Feedback
FIMS → Reports → Customer Feedback

questions.question: A customer calls claiming they submitted their prototype feedback form 40 days after delivery. The executive checks FIMS → Tools → Prototype Reviews and the form is NOT visible. Can the upgrade be processed?

options.text: Yes — the customer's claim is within the 45-day window, so process the upgrade
No — the upgrade cannot be processed regardless of what the customer claims; feedback must be visible in FIMS before any upgrade action
Yes — but escalate to Asst Manager for final confirmation before processing
No — but the executive should submit the feedback on the customer's behalf in FIMS

questions.question: A prototype customer submitted feedback using a different phone number than used during purchase. What should the executive do?

options.text: Process the upgrade — phone number matching is optional
Reject the upgrade immediately — different phone number disqualifies the customer
Verify eligibility with Manager before proceeding — this is a verification exception
Ask the customer to resubmit using the correct phone number within the original 45-day window

questions.question: What is the correct FIMS path to process a prototype upgrade?

options.text: FIMS → OMS → RMA → Create → Prototype Upgrade
FIMS → Tools → Prototype Reviews → Actions → Select Models → SKUs → Submit
FIMS → OMS → Shopify → Order → Upgrade Request → Submit
FIMS → Tools → Upgrades → Select Customer → Submit

questions.question: When selecting the SKU for a prototype upgrade in FIMS, what rule must the executive follow?

options.text: Select the SKU that the customer originally purchased — same variant
Select the most expensive SKU available as a goodwill gesture
Select the SKU based on current stock availability only — do not promise a specific SKU before verifying
Let the customer choose any SKU from any product line

questions.question: After confirming the upgrade, how many days does the customer have to ship back the prototype product?

options.text: 7 days
10 days
15 days
30 days

questions.question: Once the prototype product is received at the warehouse, within what timeline is the upgraded product dispatched?

options.text: Within 5–7 business days
Within 24–48 business hours
Within 15–20 business days
Within 10 business days

questions.question: A prototype customer contacts CRM requesting a return and full refund. What is the correct response?

options.text: Process the return — prototype watches follow the same 10-day return policy as standard products
Inform the customer that return and refund are not available for prototype watches; redirect to the Prototype Feedback Programme for a free upgrade if feedback was submitted within 45 days
Escalate to CRM Asst Manager — return/refund decisions for prototypes require manager approval
Process the refund but not the return — refunds are available for prototypes

questions.question: A prototype customer reports a manufacturing defect 6 days after delivery. What is the applicable policy?

options.text: Repair under warranty — standard warranty applies to all products
Replacement within 10 days — same model only, subject to stock availability
Free upgrade under the Prototype Feedback Programme — defects qualify automatically
Return and refund — defective products can always be returned

questions.question: A prototype customer reports a movement issue 3 months after delivery. What is the correct response?

options.text: Process as a standard warranty repair — movement is a covered component
Inform the customer that repair services for prototype watches are available only after the final version of the model is officially released; suggest the Prototype Feedback Programme
Replace the watch under the 10-day replacement policy
Escalate to CRM Asst Manager for immediate repair authorisation

questions.question: A customer asks if a UPI payment discount applies to a prototype watch purchase. What is the correct answer?

options.text: Yes — UPI discounts apply to all Sylvi products
No — no discounts of any kind apply to prototype watches, including UPI payment discounts
UPI discounts apply only if the order value exceeds ₹2,000
Escalate to Asst Manager to check current discount eligibility

questions.question: When the final version of a prototype model is launched, from what date does the warranty period start for the upgraded customer?

options.text: From the original prototype purchase date
From the date the customer submitted the feedback form
From the final edition invoice date
From the date the prototype was returned to the warehouse

questions.question: In the Prototype Feedback.xlsx tracker, what must be done with any row showing "Pending verification" in the Upgrade Eligible column?

options.text: Leave it for the weekly Asst Manager review
Escalate to Asst Manager the same day — pending verification rows cannot wait
Change to "No" after 24 hours if no response from the customer
Mark as "Yes" if the customer's feedback submission date is within 45 days

questions.question: A customer claims they submitted feedback but the form is not visible in FIMS. What is the correct escalation?

options.text: Ask the customer to resubmit and check FIMS again in 24 hours
Process the upgrade based on the customer's verbal confirmation
Escalate to Asst Manager — "feedback claimed but not visible in FIMS" is an escalation condition
Reject the upgrade permanently — no FIMS visibility means no eligibility
